name: Agent: Web Development
summary: A portable agent that turns a Figma design into a production-ready page — faithful to the design, fast, accessible, and real in every language it ships in.
objectives: Turn a Figma design into a production-ready static site that matches the design and proves it, rather than being reviewed by eye.
Keep the agent client-agnostic: every brand, endpoint and language enters through the project's own config, never through the agent.
Make quality a gate rather than an opinion — a build below the bar cannot be delivered.
Ship each language as a real page a search engine can find, not a translation swapped in by script.

outcomes.text: The agent built a landing page end to end from a single design frame and shipped it live in two languages, each one a real crawlable page set that points at the other.
The published page scores 97 / 97 / 100 / 100 on Lighthouse — performance, accessibility, best practices, SEO — against a delivery gate set at 90 in all four. Below the bar, the build does not ship.

SEO is generated from the project's own data rather than typed into the markup: one module renders the page head, the robots file and a multilingual sitemap, and a static console lets a non-developer review and edit it before launch.
Each language gets its own social preview card, written in that language — because the image a reader sees when the link is shared has words on it, and words are copy.

Fidelity is measured on three independent signals instead of one pixel percentage — because the raw number never reaches zero once photographs and substituted fonts are in the frame, and a threshold nobody can pass is a threshold everybody ignores.
The agent carries no brand of its own. A check across its core files returns zero company-specific references, so copying the folder is all it takes to run it for a different client.

milestones.title: Write the portable rule set: grid, responsive, dark mode, SEO, languages, visual QA and the pre-launch gate
Make an approved project config a hard gate — no build starts without one
Turn structural QA, visual fidelity and performance into scripts that block delivery
Build the first landing page end to end from a design frame
Ship it in two languages, each a real crawlable page set
Make SEO generated rather than hand-written, with a console a non-developer can use
Fold the first project's findings back into the rules, so the next one starts ahead of it
Set the agent up in the shared workspace so a new project can start from it
